title: Nursery Rhyme. DXVIII. Natural History.
content:
When the snow is on the ground,
            Little Robin Red-breast grieves;
        For no berries can be found,
            And on the trees there are no leaves.

        The air is cold, the worms are hid,
            For this poor bird what can be done?
        We'll strew him here some crumbs of bread,
            And then he'll live till the snow is gone.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Unknown, titled "Nursery Rhyme. DXVIII. Natural History.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
